name: Cauchy-Schwarz inequality
formulaTeX: \left| \langle u,\, v \rangle \right|^2 \;\le\; \langle u,\, u \rangle \,\langle v,\, v \rangle
conventions: <.,.> is an inner product on a real or complex vector space V
valid in any inner-product space — finite-dimensional R^n, C^n, infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces (L^2, l^2)
for the canonical Euclidean inner product on R^n: <u, v> = sum_i u_i v_i
complex case uses conjugate-symmetric inner product: <u, v> = conj(<v, u>)
expectedLimits: equality holds iff u and v are linearly dependent (one is a scalar multiple of the other)
u = 0 or v = 0: both sides equal 0 (trivial equality)
integral form (L^2): |integral f(x) g(x) dx|^2 <= integral |f|^2 dx * integral |g|^2 dx
implies the triangle inequality for any inner-product norm
references: Schwarz (1888) — modern formulation in inner-product spaces
Rudin — Real and Complex Analysis, ch.4
